finding: CLAUDE.md Principle 4 contradicts ADR-018 (Accepted). CLAUDE.md says 'One work item at a time'; ADR-018 clarifies it as 'one per operator'.
detail: ADR-018 explicitly references and re-states Principle 4 but CLAUDE.md has not been updated. For multi-operator teams the current wording implies repo-wide serialisation, contradicting the accepted ADR.
recommendation: Update CLAUDE.md Principle 4 to 'One workstream per operator at a time' (or equivalent) and link to ADR-018. WORK-0009 tracks this — confirm it is unblocked and not just deferred.

finding: ADR-017 (Accepted) names existential-challenger as a mandatory reviewer for `.work/decisions/*.md`; dispatch-rules.json only lists historian for that pattern.
detail: ADR-017 acknowledges the promotion as a follow-up (tracked by WORK-0005), so this is not a strict contradiction, but the gate is currently aspirational: new ADRs can be written without existential-challenger review.
recommendation: Either land WORK-0005 quickly or add a temporary rule routing the library prompt for that path so the gate is not silently bypassed.

finding: ADR-019 (Accepted) names 'workstream' the unit of work, but CLAUDE.md, SKILL.md, references, and .work/work.json still use 'work item' / 'phase' freely.
detail: Both terminologies coexist without a glossary mapping. WORK-0015 tracks the audit; the contradiction is acknowledged as deferred.
recommendation: Acceptable as deferred work; pair WORK-0015 with WORK-0010 (recursive schema) so terminology and schema migrate together.

finding: agents.md says 'Seven core agents' and tiers.md says '19 additional reviewer prompts'; these counts become wrong as soon as WORK-0005 lands.
detail: Pre-existing valid claim; flagged so docs updates are bundled with WORK-0005.
recommendation: Update both files when WORK-0005 merges (already in the acceptance criteria).

finding: ADR-002 makes the gate the only mandatory artefact across all tiers; budget_manager.py creates .work/ unconditionally even in gate tier.
detail: Already tracked as WORK-0002; flagged as a live contradiction so it gets early phase-1 priority.
